capstone-metric-map.label: Building a Metric Map
capstone-metric-map.summary: Plan how each report column will be calculated and validated.

capstone_metric_map_step4.title: Sales Performance by Region and Status

capstone_metric_map_step1.title: Calculate Total Sales
capstone_metric_map_step1.prompt: Start the final capstone project. In step 1 of 5, Write a query to calculate the total sales for each region. Use the `sales_reporting` table and consider the `quantity` and `unit_price` columns.
capstone_metric_map_step1.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

capstone_metric_map_step2.title: Average Customer Rating by Category
capstone_metric_map_step2.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 5, Write a query to find the average customer rating for each product category. Use the `sales_reporting` table and handle any null values appropriately.
capstone_metric_map_step2.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

capstone_metric_map_step2.starterCode:
SELECT region, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY region;
-- Capstone step 2: Average Customer Rating by Category
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to find the average customer rating for each product category
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

main_py.content:
SELECT region, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY region;
-- Capstone step 2: Average Customer Rating by Category
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to find the average customer rating for each product category
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

capstone_metric_map_step3.title: Identify Top Sales Rep
capstone_metric_map_step3.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 3 of 5, Write a query to identify the sales representative with the highest total sales. Use the `sales_reporting` table and consider both `quantity` and `unit_price`.
capstone_metric_map_step3.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

capstone_metric_map_step3.starterCode:
SELECT category, AVG(COALESCE(customer_rating, 0)) AS avg_rating
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category;
-- Capstone step 3: Identify Top Sales Rep
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to identify the sales representative with the highest total sales
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.
capstone_metric_map_step3.solutionCode:
SELECT sales_rep, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY sales_rep
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 1;

main_py.content:
SELECT category, AVG(COALESCE(customer_rating, 0)) AS avg_rating
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY category;
-- Capstone step 3: Identify Top Sales Rep
-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to identify the sales representative with the highest total sales
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

SELECT sales_rep, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY sales_rep
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 1;

capstone_metric_map_step4.title: Sales Performance by Region and Status
capstone_metric_map_step4.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 4 of 5, Write a query to calculate the total sales for each region and order status. Use the `sales_reporting` table and group the results accordingly.
capstone_metric_map_step4.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.

capstone_metric_map_step4.starterCode:
SELECT sales_rep, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY sales_rep
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 1;

-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to calculate the total sales for each region and order status

main_py.content:

capstone_metric_map_step5.title: Conditional Sales Analysis
capstone_metric_map_step5.prompt: Continue the final capstone project from the previous working step. In step 5 of 5, Write a query to calculate the total sales for orders with a discount greater than 5%. Use the `sales_reporting` table and filter the results accordingly.
capstone_metric_map_step5.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: The final capstone is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.
help.hint_2: Run the previous working code first, then make only the focused change for this step.

capstone_metric_map_step5.starterCode:
SELECT region, order_status, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales
FROM sales_reporting
GROUP BY region, order_status;

-- Keep the working code above from the previous step.
-- Next, Write a query to calculate the total sales for orders with a discount greater than 5%
-- Add only the focused change for this step inside the existing work.

main_py.content:

FROM sales_reporting

capstone-metric-map-sketch0.title: Capstone Project Overview
capstone-metric-map-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: In this final capstone project, you will create an Executive Sales Performance Report using the `sales_reporting` table from the `sales_kpi` dataset. Your task is to integrate various SQL concepts such as aggregate functions, conditional aggregation, and null-safe calculations to produce a comprehensive report. The report should answer key business questions and provide insights into sales performance across different regions and categories. You will progressively build this report, ensuring each step is validated and explained in business terms.
